channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: College Baseball Player Ejected During National Anthem for Rule Enforcement Controversy
item.description: During a college baseball game on April 4, 2026, between the San Diego Toreros and the Santa Clara Broncos, an unusual ejection sparked widespread controversy. Jagger Edwards, a San Diego player, was ...

item.title: University of Fort Hare anti‑corruption drive takes personal toll as VC faces suspension and family breaks down
item.description: The University of Fort Hare vice‑chancellor, Professor Sakhela Buhlungu, has been suspended by the institution’s council pending disciplinary action, amid a broader crisis over irregular appointments ...

item.title: Mother: Stories from the NGV Collection Explores the Realities of Motherhood
item.description: The National Gallery of Victoria's new exhibition, 'Mother: Stories from the NGV Collection', explores the complex, multifaceted experiences of motherhood through more than 200 works. Curated by Sophi...

item.title: IEBC outlines voter registration targets ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has identified key battleground areas across Kenya as preparations for the 2027 General Election gather pace. According to the electoral body...

item.title: Balochistan and KP impose early closing times to reduce energy consumption
item.description: The provincial governments of Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) have introduced new measures to conserve energy by setting fixed closing times for markets, restaurants, and wedding venues. Under...

item.title: Tanzania Police Dismiss False Rumours of Men Losing Genitals, Confirm Five Deaths from Mob Attacks
item.description: The Tanzania Police Force has strongly warned citizens against spreading false rumours claiming that men are losing their private parts after being tapped on the shoulder by strangers. According to Po...

item.title: ACT Wazalendo criticizes TCRA for suspending Jambo TV licence, urges government to protect press freedom
item.description: ACT Wazalendo, one of Tanzania's political parties, has openly criticised the Tanzania Communications Regulatory Authority (TCRA) for suspending Jambo TV’s licence. The suspension, announced on April ...

item.title: Leeds lead West Ham in FA Cup quarter-final as Farke balances league survival and cup ambition
item.description: Leeds United took an early lead against West Ham in the FA Cup quarter-final at London Stadium, with Ao Tanaka scoring the decisive goal after a well-worked move. The match has been closely contested,...

item.title: Conflicting Reports Surround U.S. Rescue Mission of Downed Pilot in Iran
item.description: On April 5, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that American forces had carried out a high-risk mission to rescue a downed airman whose F-15E fighter jet crashed in southwestern Iran. The inc...

item.title: Nishanthi appointed as the new District Collector of Alluri Sitarama Raju
item.description: In a recent administrative development, Ms. Nishanthi officially took charge as the new District Collector of Alluri Sitarama Raju (ASR) district in Andhra Pradesh. The ceremonial handover took place ...

item.title: Blue Jays make roster moves: Little sent to Triple-A, Santander and Ponce on 60-day injured list
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays have announced roster changes as part of their early-season adjustments. Pitcher Jordan Little has been optioned to Triple-A Buffalo, signalling a temporary move to provide him w...

item.title: UP extends deadline for UPCAT applications to April 13, 2026
item.description: The University of the Philippines has officially extended the application period for the UPCAT (UP College Admission Test) to April 13, 2026. Originally, the deadline was March 30. This extension appl...

item.title: Airport security staff caught stealing ₱7,000 from Australian tourist at NAIA
item.description: An Australian tourist traveling to Cebu reported to airport police that ₱7,000 in cash went missing from her shoulder bag during a routine table inspection at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (N...

item.title: Family donates Aberfan victims’ personal items to Welsh national museum
item.description: Ahead of the 60th anniversary of the Aberfan disaster, the family of 10‑year‑old Marylyn Minett have donated her school exercise book and other personal items to Amgueddfa Cymru, Wales’s national muse...

item.title: Baptisms and Pollution: Communities Continue Spiritual Rituals Along Johannesburg’s Contaminated Klip River
item.description: For many Johannesburg residents, the Klip River remains a spiritual sanctuary despite being dangerously polluted. Every Sunday, people gather along its banks in Soweto and Lenasia to perform baptisms ...

item.title: PM Modi Criticizes TMC Over Malda Incident
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has strongly criticised the Trinamool Congress (TMC) over an incident in Malda, West Bengal. He termed the situation as an example of 'maha-jungleraj' – a term suggesting ...

item.title: President Ruto Encourages Kenyans to Stay Hopeful and United During Easter
item.description: On Good Friday, President William Ruto sent out a message to all Kenyans, urging them to embrace hope, unity and faith even when facing challenges. Speaking from Nairobi, he reflected on the meaning o...

item.title: Rivers Police Strengthen Human Rights and Community Policing Efforts
item.description: The Rivers State Commissioner of Police, Olugbenga Adepoju, don reassure people say the command dey committed to protecting human rights and giving better police service wey go benefit everybody. He t...

item.title: US reallocates stealth cruise missile stockpiles in preparation for expanded Iran campaign
item.description: The United States has reportedly moved to commit nearly its entire supply of stealthy JASSM-ER cruise missiles as part of its widening military operations against Iran. The decision, made at the end o...

item.title: Trump criticised for aggressive message on Iran during Easter Sunday
item.description: US President Donald Trump sparked widespread criticism on Easter Sunday with a forceful message directed at Iran, threatening severe military action if the Strait of Hormuz remained blocked. He warned...
